UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the VoltCare application. This entity stores personal details, language preferences, and a link to a profile picture.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

username.description: A unique username chosen by the user.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and potentially login.

phoneNumber.description: The user's phone number, used for communication and potentially login.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for the application interface (e.g., English, Hindi, Odia).

Complaint.title: Complaint
Complaint.description: Stores details of a user-submitted complaint regarding utility services, including location, problem category, and tracking status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Complaint entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who submitted the complaint. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Complaint)

utilityType.description: The type of utility the complaint pertains to (e.g., Electricity, Water).

town.description: The town where the complaint originated (e.g., Baripada).

wardNumber.description: The specific ward number within the town related to the complaint (e.g., 1 to 31).

localityAddress.description: Detailed locality or affected address where the problem occurred.

problemCategory.description: The specific category of the problem (e.g., Frequent power cuts, Voltage fluctuation, Damaged electric wires, Transformer failure).

description.description: An optional detailed description provided by the user about the problem.

status.description: The current status of the complaint (e.g., Submitted, In Progress, Resolved).

submittedAt.description: Timestamp when the complaint was initially submitted.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the complaint's status or details were last updated.

resolutionDetails.description: Optional details provided by an administrator upon resolution of the complaint.

UtilityBill.title: UtilityBill
UtilityBill.description: Records details of a user's utility bill, including billing period, amount, payment status, and links to relevant documents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UtilityBill entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile to whom the bill belongs. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UtilityBill)

utilityType.description: The type of utility service (e.g., Electricity, Water).

departmentName.description: The name of the utility department (e.g., EHT (O&M) Division, Baripada).

consumerAccountNumber.description: The consumer or account number associated with the utility service.

billingPeriodStart.description: The start date of the billing period for this bill.

billingPeriodEnd.description: The end date of the billing period for this bill.

billAmount.description: The total amount of the bill.

paymentStatus.description: The current payment status of the bill (e.g., Paid, Pending, Failed).

dueDate.description: The due date for the bill payment.

paymentId.description: Optional reference ID from the payment gateway or system for the transaction.

paidAt.description: Timestamp when the bill was successfully paid.

billDocumentUrl.description: URL to the detailed bill document (e.g., PDF) for viewing or download.

receiptDocumentUrl.description: URL to the payment receipt document (e.g., PDF) for viewing or download.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Each document represents a single user's profile and is accessible only by the owning user matching `{userId}`. The document's 'id' field is denormalized to match '{userId}'.
A subcollection holding complaints submitted by a specific user. Primarily for user-centric access and tracking. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who submitted the complaint.

params.name: complaintId
params.description: The unique identifier for the complaint.

definition.description: A top-level collection containing all submitted complaints, primarily for administrative viewing and management. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence and efficient QAPs for administrators.

params.name: complaintId
params.description: The unique identifier for the complaint.

definition.description: A subcollection holding utility bill records for a specific user. Accessible only by the owning user matching `{userId}`. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user to whom the bill belongs.

params.name: billId
params.description: The unique identifier for the utility bill.

definition.description: Identifies users who have administrative privileges. Used for DBAC (Database Access Control) where document existence grants a role. The document itself may be empty or contain minimal data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to meet the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and efficient Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) for both users and administrators. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**

1.  **User Profiles (`/userProfiles/{userId}`):** Each user's profile is stored in a top-level collection. Access is strictly controlled by `request.auth.uid` matching the `{userId}` in the path. The UserProfile document itself will also contain an `id` field matching the `userId` for explicit consistency, eliminating any need for `get()` operations to check ownership.
2.  **User-Owned Subcollections (`/users/{userId}/complaints/{complaintId}` and `/users/{userId}/utilityBills/{billId}`):** For data strongly owned by a user (complaints they filed, bills they need to pay), we utilize subcollections under a user-specific path. The `{userId}` in the path directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, making authorization rules atomic and independent of parent documents. Both `Complaint` and `UtilityBill` documents will also contain a `userId` field, denormalizing ownership directly into the document. This is critical for robust atomic operations and simplified rule logic.
3.  **Global Complaints for Admin (`/complaints/{complaintId}`):** To address the requirement for administrators to manage *all* complaints, a top-level `complaints` collection is created. Each complaint document in this collection will also contain the `userId` of the submitter, as well as `status`, `submittedAt`, etc. This denormalization means that an admin rule checking for administrative privileges (via `/roles_admin/{userId}`) does not need to perform a `get()` call to the user's subcollection or `UserProfile` to view complaint details. All necessary authorization and context (like the submitter's ID) are present directly within the `Complaint` document.
4.  **Admin Roles (`/roles_admin/{userId}`):** Admin roles are established using the DBAC (Database Access Control) pattern. The mere existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{request.auth.uid}` grants administrative privileges, making authorization checks extremely simple and independent of any other data. Rules can directly check `get(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)).exists()`.

**Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs):**

1.  **User-Specific Lists:** For users, listing their complaints (`/users/{request.auth.uid}/complaints`) or bills (`/users/{request.auth.uid}/utilityBills`) naturally adheres to QAPs. The `list` operation is restricted by the path, and rules ensure that `request.auth.uid` matches the path's `userId`. This prevents broad, unfiltered queries that could lead to security vulnerabilities.
2.  **Admin-Specific Lists:** For administrators, the `/complaints` collection allows them to `list` all complaints efficiently. The security rule for this collection would grant `list` access if the `request.auth.uid` is identified as an admin (e.g., `get(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)).exists()`). Because the rule directly grants permission to list *all* documents in this collection for an admin, it fully supports QAPs without requiring filters in the security rules themselves.

**Denormalization Justification:** The key denormalization strategy is applied to the `Complaint` entity. A complaint filed by a user will be written to two locations: 
    a.  `/users/{userId}/complaints/{complaintId}`: This ensures strong ownership for the user, simplifying user-facing rules and reads.
    b.  `/complaints/{complaintId}`: This global collection allows administrators to efficiently query and manage *all* complaints from a single location without needing to fan out queries across all user subcollections. The `userId` field within each `Complaint` document in this global collection allows for comprehensive display and context, enabling rules to function without cross-collection `get()` operations.

This structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable rules by making authorization context explicit, segregating data by its security posture, and ensuring that `list` operations are always secure and performant.
